exampleSentences: Bài kiểm tra hôm nay cực khó nên em làm mãi mới xong.
Anh cực thích món phở ở quán này vì nó rất ngon.
Thời tiết ngoài trời hôm nay cực lạnh, nhớ mặc áo ấm nhé.

definitions.en: extreme or utmost; the highest point or degree.
definitions.vi: cực là cực độ hoặc mức độ cao nhất.
definitions.es: extremo o máximo; el punto o grado más alto.

example.en: Mount Everest is the highest peak in the world.
example.vi: Núi Everest là đỉnh núi cao nhất trên thế giới.
example.es: El Monte Everest es el pico más alto del mundo.

translation.en: The weather is extremely cold today.

notes_md:
**cực** = *extremely / super* (very strong emphasis). It makes adjectives or feelings more intense than **rất**.

It usually goes **before adjectives or verbs** to boost them.  

It’s informal and punchy — stronger and more emotional than *rất*.  
Synonyms for memory: **rất**, **vô cùng** (more neutral/formal).  
It can also mean “pole” (like **Bắc Cực** = North Pole), but the intensifier meaning is what you’ll hear most in daily speech.
notes:
cực = extremely / super (very strong emphasis). It makes adjectives or feelings more intense than rất.

It usually goes before adjectives or verbs to boost them.  

It’s informal and punchy — stronger and more emotional than rất.  
Synonyms for memory: rất, vô cùng (more neutral/formal).  
It can also mean “pole” (like Bắc Cực = North Pole), but the intensifier meaning is what you’ll hear most in daily speech.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Today's test was extremely difficult so I took a long time to finish.
I extremely like the pho at this restaurant because it is very delicious.
The weather outside today is extremely cold, remember to wear warm clothes.

translation.en: This is an extremely interesting thing.

content.sentence: Đây là một điều ___ thú vị.
